item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Weird… The date that Dr. Pike got her closure was my birthday, on the same year my cat Kaylee died.

Anyways, I loved every part of this. Site-87 seems like such a cool place to be.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here from Spook Creep Pester and I have to say I love the tie in.
+1

item.title: Best Scp ever
item.content:encoded:
this is my favorite scp and it brought tears to my eyes. I love It
+1

item.title: In Memoriam
item.content:encoded: This article is dedicated to Mia, the sweetest cat I've ever known. Rest in peace, skinny minny, you beautiful cat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creative idea, good writing, good pacing, the only problem I have with this is that the foundation isn't typically so nice. Other than that, this is a very solid SCP

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hey I'm crying why would you do this to me

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I realized who this was halfway through. Good cat, best friend. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suddenly laughed when I realized it was a Sloth Spit skip. I was like "Of course it is! Of course it is…" I don't know why I didn't catch on until the name-drop… you'd think the term "whammied" would have been a dead giveaway.

Anyway, this is a surprisingly well-crafted skip that reads like a Tale. Upvoted. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This turned out quite well! While I'm normally not one for SCPs evoking touching emotions, this was so well written I had to like it. The emotions expressed in it felt legitimate and the narrative it tells is one of the best I've read here in a while.

+1 (and apologies for some bad crit I gave this on chat a few months back)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The arc is paced so well. An important thing to remember is that the Foundation is cold, but the people who work there are still human. You found a fantastic balance. Upvoted without a doubt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was Bast-tastic. I like SCPs that don't kill people, and this actually helped someone. I agree with DrSevere - this humanizes the staff. The Foundation is cold, but this is less cold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to see this one posted, I remember reading it a while back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a tough one.

This is one of the rare SCPs that aims for an emotion other than fear or curiosity. It's heartfelt and touching and I like that.

With that said, it feels a little too nice for my taste. Everything works out fine, it really was just trying to help…plus, it pushed my suspension of disbelief a bit that the Foundation would be fine with her just playing with a cat after clearly developing an emotional attachment. Again, though, this humanizes the entire research staff in a way that really appeals to me, so that's sort of a point in its favor.

After much consideration and internal debate, I think I like more about this than I don't. +1.

item.title: Meow-thur Paw-mentary (I'm so sorry)
item.content:encoded:
This has been in the works for a little while, well over two months, I think. And it's the first skip I've posted since the 3000 contest. Oliver in this is based off of a cat I owned of the same name, who vanished two years ago. This is dedicated to him, as sappy as that sounds.

This is set in the S & C Plastics universe, and as far as I'm concerned, is canon within it. Dr. Pike, Dr. Mattings and Dr. West have all appeared in at least one tale in S & C P, as has Agent Ewell. Dr. Kola is the only one I haven't written into the canon yet.

Special thanks to SunnyClockwork and all others that helped critique this (my brain's frazzled from heat and travel, so if you helped to critique this, tell me and I'll add you here.)
